name: Sarvam-105B
archDisplay: 105B MoE transformer (128 experts, ~10.3B active/token), Multi-head Latent Attention
note: India's first sovereign 100B+ open model, trained from scratch under the IndiaAI Mission; Apache-2.0 MoE with Multi-head Latent Attention and 128K context via YaRN, covering 22 Indian languages plus English.
tpsNote: Too large to run on a single RTX 4090 even at Q4 (~64GB needed); no consumer single-GPU llama.cpp benchmark found.

results.notes: Arena Hard v2, from the model card's 'Knowledge & Coding' benchmark table
IFEval, from the model card's 'Knowledge & Coding' benchmark table

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real, very recent release — independently corroborated by Bloomberg ('Upstart Sarvam Unveils AI Model Customized for India Market', 2026-02-18), TechCrunch, Business Standard, and BusinessToday, all reporting weights publicly available since 2026-02-18 at the India AI Impact Summit (a later Sarvam blog post dated ~2026-03-06 appears to be a secondary/formal write-up, not the actual release date). Architecture (MoE, 128 experts, Multi-head Latent Attention, 10.3B active, 128K context via YaRN factor 40) and all 8 result rows literally confirmed on https://huggingface.co/sarvamai/sarvam-105b and the official Sarvam blog. Apache 2.0 license confirmed. Minor note: HF safetensors metadata reports actual weight size as '106B params' though the model is uniformly named/marketed as '105B' by Sarvam and all press — paramsB=105 kept per canonical naming. No corrections made to core fields.

2026-07-16: Resolved three flagged core-benchmark gaps for Sarvam-105B. (1) hle — ADDED: Sarvam never self-reports an HLE score, but Artificial Analysis independently evaluated the model (as "Sarvam 105B (high)") for its Intelligence Index v4.1 and measured Humanity's Last Exam at ~10% (https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/sarvam-105b, corroborated at 10.1% by aggregator BenchLM.ai citing the same AA data at https://benchlm.ai/models/sarvam-105b); this is a genuine independent third-party eval, not a self-report, so it is added as source="independent". (2) humaneval — OMITTED, no evidence found: checked the official HF model card/blog (its "Knowledge & Coding" table reports LiveCodeBench v6 instead of HumanEval), independent leaderboards (Artificial Analysis substitutes SciCode for HumanEval and does not track it; Vals.ai and Epoch.ai do not list any Sarvam model at all), and the sibling Sarvam-30B model card, which DOES report HumanEval=92.1 — but that figure belongs to the smaller 30B model, not the 105B, and cannot be carried over. Sarvam appears to have deliberately dropped HumanEval from the 105B's own benchmark table in favor of the harder LiveCodeBench v6, consistent with HumanEval being treated as a saturated legacy benchmark at this model's scale (the 30B sibling already scores 92.1). Genuine absence, not a research gap. (3) mmmu — OMITTED, not applicable: Sarvam-105B is a text-only model (modalities: ["text"], capabilities.vision: false); Sarvam's multimodal/document-vision work lives in a separate "Sarvam Vision" 3B model, so an MMMU (multimodal) score was never expected to exist for this model and none was found in any source checked.
